commands.review_summary:
The PR vendors a new `.agents/skills/autoreview` skill with a Python review helper and bash harness for bundle-based Codex, Claude, Droid, and Copilot code reviews.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new repo-local skill PR, not a reported bug. I smoke-checked the proposed helper on a downloaded copy with `--help`, `--dry-run`, and `py_compile`, but there is no failing current-main behavior to reproduce.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This member-authored draft adds new agent automation and needs maintainer validation and ownership review rather than an automated repair.

target.title: chore(agents): add autoreview skill
target.body:
## Summary
- vendor the OpenClaw autoreview skill into `.agents/skills/autoreview`
- adapt the helper for this repo's `master` default branch
- harden review bundles to avoid local path leakage, unsafe symlink dereferencing, unbounded dirty prompts, and unsandboxed non-Codex tools by default

## Validation
- `python3 -m py_compile .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/master --dry-run`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search --prompt "Review the vendored OpenClaw Windows Node autoreview skill adaptation after preserving regular-file sides of symlink typechanges. Focus on publish safety and concrete correctness defects only. Do not flag broad speculative design changes."` -> clean
- blocked locally: `./build.ps1` / shared tests / tray tests because this mac lane has no `pwsh` or `dotnet`
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
